questions.q: What was Jeff Bezos's yearly salary from Amazon in early April 2026, according to Fortune?
questions.correctText: Amazon still paid Jeff Bezos an $80,000 yearly salary, with an additional $1.6 million allocated for travel and security expenses in early April 2026.

questions.q: How much in stock did Oracle's new CFO receive shortly after layoffs, as reported by Yahoo Finance?
questions.correctText: Oracle's new CFO received $26 million in stock following a period of layoffs, with an employee suggesting an 'algorithm' may have targeted workers with stock options for dismissal.

questions.q: According to CNBC, what did Warren Buffett say about his past Apple stock sales in late March 2026?
options: He sold it too soon and would buy more in a different market
He believes Apple's stock is overvalued
He plans to sell all his remaining Apple stock
questions.correctText: Warren Buffett stated in late March 2026 that he sold Apple stock "too soon" and would be inclined to buy more of it, though not in the current market conditions.

questions.q: Which two entities are reportedly nearing a critical minerals deal to counter Chinese control, as reported by Bloomberg on April 10, 2026?
questions.correctText: The European Union and the United States are reportedly close to finalizing a critical minerals deal aimed at combating Chinese control over these resources.

questions.q: According to Reuters on April 13, 2026, what is the Chinese AI startup StepFun planning to do to facilitate an IPO?
options: Relocate its headquarters to the US
questions.correctText: Chinese AI startup StepFun is reportedly planning to unwind its offshore structure to pave the way for an Initial Public Offering (IPO).

questions.q: How much additional funding did Japan approve for chipmaker Rapidus on April 11, 2026, as reported by Reuters?
questions.correctText: Japan approved an additional $4 billion in funding for the chipmaker Rapidus on April 11, 2026.

questions.q: What was Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's total pay in 2025, with climbing security and travel costs, as reported by Business Insider in April 2026?
questions.correctText: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's pay rose to $2.1 million in 2025, largely due to increased costs for security and travel.
